name: La Morranda and Els Estrets Trail
name_original: La Morranada — Circular por el barranco de Els Estrets
description: Hike the easy 2.7-mile (4.4 km) La Morranada circular route through Els Estrets ravine, featuring gorges, unique geology, and the Toll Blau.
region_intro: Hiking around La Pobla De Benifassà provides access to diverse natural landscapes within the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park, featuring jagged rocks, lush woodlands, and clear waters. The region is characterized by gorges such as Bel and La Font d'Enròs, extensive forests of Aleppo pine, black pine, and evergreen oaks, and significant water features like the Ulldecona Reservoir. Notable natural landmarks include the 30-meter high El Salt de Robert waterfall and the rocky formations of Portell de l'Infern. This area offers a variety of terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around La Pobla De Benifassà The most popular hiking route is Ulldecona Reservoir and Moleta del Terme Loop, a 5.6 miles (8.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This route explores the scenic Ulldecona Reservoir and the surrounding terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Portell de l'Infern - Ulldecona Reservoir Trail, a difficult 11.2 miles (18.1 km) path. This trail offers views of the Portell de l'Infern rock formations and the Ulldecona Reservoir. Local hikers also love the Portell de l'Infern and Salt de Robert Loop, a 9.2 miles (14.9 km) trail leading through Portell de l'Infern and past the Salt de Robert waterfall, often completed in about 4 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around La Pobla De Benifassà is defined by diverse forests, gorges, and the Ulldecona Reservoir. The extensive network of trails offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels. The routes in La Pobla De Benifassà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 1100 hikers have used komoot to explore La Pobla De Benifassà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around La Pobla De Benifassà?
A: There are over 60 hiking trails around La Pobla De Benifassà. These include 15 easy routes, 40 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels within the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park.

Q: Are there any notable waterfalls to see while hiking in the area?
A: Yes, the area features &lt;strong&gt;El Salt de Robert&lt;/strong&gt;, a striking 30-meter high waterfall. You can experience it on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880461994/portell-de-l-infern-and-salt-de-robert-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portell de l'Infern and Salt de Robert Loop&lt;/a&gt;, or the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880413771/ombries-del-salt-de-robert-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ombries del Salt de Robert Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails around La Pobla De Benifassà?
A: The terrain around La Pobla De Benifassà is diverse, ranging from lush woodlands with Aleppo pine, black pine, and evergreen oaks, to jagged rocky formations and gorges. Many trails follow ancient paths connecting villages within the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in La Pobla De Benifassà?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880449930/ulldecona-reservoir-and-moleta-del-terme-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir and Moleta del Terme Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the scenic reservoir and surrounding landscape. The region also features the 'Seven Pobles Route', a significant circular trail connecting the seven villages of Tinença de Benifassà.

Q: What are some interesting natural landmarks or viewpoints to look out for?
A: Hikers can explore the dramatic rocky caves of &lt;strong&gt;Portell de l'Infern&lt;/strong&gt;, which offer spectacular panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3425404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another significant attraction, known for its magnificent views and water that shifts between emerald and turquoise hues.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in the area?
A: Yes, there are 15 easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880427605/la-morranda-and-els-estrets-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morranda and Els Estrets Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, covering about 4.4 km with moderate elevation gain, making it a good choice for a family outing.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in La Pobla De Benifassà?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 180 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the tranquility of the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park, and the variety of trails available for different skill levels.

Q: Can I find any historical sites or cultural attractions along the trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, the region offers historical points of interest. You can find ancient rock art at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4623502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fenellasa Rock Paintings&lt;/a&gt; near the Ulldecona reservoir dam, which are a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The Monasterio de Santa María de Benifassà, founded in 1233, is also historically significant.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Pobla De Benifassà?
A: La Pobla De Benifassà is an excellent destination for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but the diverse forests provide shade, and the Ulldecona Reservoir offers refreshing views. Winter hiking is also possible, with the landscape taking on a different, tranquil beauty.

Q: Are there any specific gorges or canyons that are popular for hiking?
A: The village itself is situated between the Bel gorge and La Font d'Enròs gorge. The Barranc de la Fo is another narrow gorge characterized by high rocky walls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parrissal Walkways&lt;/a&gt; offer a unique experience through gorges along the Matarranya River, with wooden pathways.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging hikes?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, there are 9 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e951826985/portell-de-l-infern-ulldecona-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portell de l'Infern - Ulldecona Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 18.1 km path, offering significant elevation changes and rewarding views of the Portell de l'Infern rock formations and the Ulldecona Reservoir.

Q: Where can I find accommodation or places to rest near the hiking trails?
A: The region offers various options for accommodation, including rural houses and hostels in La Pobla De Benifassà and surrounding villages. There are also several huts and refuges, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5092818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Forestal de la Tenalla&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4980710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font Ferrera Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, which can be useful for longer treks.
tips: Although the town is practically destroyed, some walls and plots can still be seen. It offers very nice views over the river. It can be easily reached with children from the town of El Ballestar. faqs.question: Is the La Morranada — Circular through the Els Estrets ravine suitable for beginners and families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route is generally considered an easy hike, making it suitable for beginners and families. It lacks technical difficulty, with mostly accessible paths, and is a great way to experience the natural beauty of the area.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the La Morranada circular trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available near the trailhead. During high season, access to Estrets d'Arnes may be regulated, and a parking fee could apply. It's advisable to check local conditions before your visit.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the La Morranada trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs within Els Ports Natural Park can vary. It's always best to check the park's official guidelines regarding pets, especially leash requirements, to ensure a pleasant and compliant visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but the gorge provides some shade, and the Els Estrets Gorge and Toll Blau offer refreshing spots. Always check weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park?
faqs.answer: While the trail is located within Els Ports Natural Park, if you plan to visit the nearby Tinença de Benifassà Natural Park, it's generally free to enter. However, specific activities or access to certain areas might require permits or have fees. Always consult the official park website for the most current regulations regarding permits or entrance fees.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the La Morranada circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and engaging. You'll navigate through a stunning gorge with vertical walls, alongside the Estrets River, and through pine forests, bushes, and meadows. The area features fascinating karstic relief, including eroded limestone formations, natural water outlets, and small caves.

faqs.question: What natural features and points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several highlights. You'll pass through the impressive Els Estrets Gorge, known for its dramatic rock formations. A notable spot is Toll Blau, the only pool where swimming is permitted. You can also visit the La Morranda Iberian settlement, adding a historical dimension to your hike.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for wild swimming on this trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, while swimming is generally restricted in most pools along the Estrets River, the Toll Blau is a notable exception. It offers a shallow and picturesque spot for a refreshing dip, making it a perfect reward during warmer months.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares approximately 63% of its path with sections of the Pedals dels Ports trail, offering a glimpse into a longer, more extensive cycling and hiking route in the region.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in the Els Ports Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The diverse ecosystem of Els Ports Natural Park provides opportunities to observe local wildlife. Keep an eye out for royal eagles soaring above and mountain goats on the rocky slopes. The area is a protected wildlife reserve, increasing your chances of spotting various species.

faqs.question: Is there a recreational area nearby for picnics or rest?
faqs.answer: Yes, the La Franqueta Recreational Area is located alongside the Els Estrets river. It provides an ideal spot for picnics and rest, and can also serve as an alternative starting point for longer walks in the area.
